titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Bradley Smith Collection of Henry Miller
titlestmt.author: by Nora Soto

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday January 9, 2025 at 00:59

did.unittitle: Bradley Smith collection of Henry Miller

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains correspondence, writings, photographs, and other papers relating to the books My Life and Times and Insomnia: or, The Devil at Large , written by Henry Miller and published by Bradley Smith's imprint Gemini Smith, Inc.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, writings, photographs, and other papers relating to the books My Life and Times and Insomnia: or, The Devil at Large , written by Henry Miller and published by Bradley Smith's imprint Gemini Smith, Inc. Correspondence consists of letters from Miller, Smith, photographers, publishers, and others involved in the books' publications. Other correspondents include Miller's friend Emil White. Writings include autograph manuscript drafts by Miller, as well as notes, drafts, galley proofs, and page proofs. Photographs include photographic prints, negatives, and slides for both books.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. March 2018 Acquisition, 1968–1978. II. July 2018 Acquisition, 1970–1976.

bioghist.head: Bradley Smith (1910–1997)
bioghist.p: Bradley Smith was an American photographer, author, and publisher who was a friend of Henry Miller. Under his imprint Gemini Smith, Inc., Smith published two books by Miller: My Life and Times (1971) and Insomnia: or, The Devil at Large (1974). Born on June 30, 1910 in New Orleans, Louisiana and raised in Karnes City, Texas, Smith worked as a freelance photographer for magazines such as Click , The Saturday Evening Post and Time in the 1930s and 1940s. He subsequently became a staff photographer for Life . In 1944, he co-founded the American Society of Magazine Photographers, a trade association and advocacy group. From the 1950s to 1980s Smith authored over twenty books, including the series A History in Art , as well as Erotic Art of the Masters (1970) and The American Way of Sex (1978). He died on August 28, 1997 in La Jolla, California.

bioghist.head: Henry Miller (1891–1980)
bioghist.p: Henry Miller was an American author and painter. A full biographical note for Henry Miller is provided in the finding aid for the Henry Miller Papers (YCAL MSS 472).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Glenn Horowitz Bookseller, Inc. on the Sinclair Lewis Fund and the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2018.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Bradley Smith Collection of Henry Miller. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Bradley Smith Collection of Henry Miller is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below, identified by a unique call number and titled according to month and year of acquisition.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing. Information in brackets is also supplied by staff.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Printed material received with the collection was removed for separate cataloging and can be accessed by searching the library's online catalog.

persname.part: Miller, Henry, 1891-1980 (Insomnia : or, The devil at large)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence, writings, production files, and photographs relating to Miller's book My Life and Times , which was published by Smith's imprint Gemini Smith, Inc. in 1971. Correspondence includes letters exchanged between Miller and Smith, and letters from Miller's secretaries, Smith's assistants, photographers, and publishers. Also present are letters from American writer and artist Emil White, a friend of Miller. Writings include notes, drafts, galley proofs, and dummies. There are also photographic prints, negatives, and slides collected for the book.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under three headings: Correspondence, Writings, and Photographs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: The World of Henry Miller , galley proof

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Henry Miller with secretary; with Bradley; humor shots

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence, writings, production files, and photographs relating to Miller's book Insomnia: or, the Devil at Large , which was published by Smith's imprint Gemini Smith, Inc. in 1974. Correspondence includes Smith's letters to Miller, and letters exchanged between Smith, Emil White, and others. Writings include autograph manuscripts by Miller and a signed colophon page from a previous printing by Loujon Press. There are also drafts, page proofs, and a small amount of photographic prints and negatives collected for the book.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under two headings: Correspondence and Writings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.
